latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,813 · day +0.57% · SPY +0.38% · alpha +0.20% — up AND beat the market
latest_messages.body: A good day — we finished +0.57% while the S&P 500 rose +0.38%, so we made money AND outran the market by about 0.2 points. No trades: no buying, selling, or adjustments; every safety check stayed clean all day. Why we won today: our specific stocks did the work — GE and Eli Lilly, two of our biggest holdings, climbed harder than the market average, which is the whole point of owning a few high-quality names. Deere even recovered from a small dip to finish essentially flat. All four holdings are on trailing safety nets: GE +9.3%, LLY +7.2%, JPM +5.2%, DE -0.2% (Deere's cushion tightest at ~2.3% but holding fine). GE reports earnings tomorrow morning — the one event I'm watching; I'll hold through it and let its trailing stop protect the +9.3% gain. Still ~44% cash (0 of 3 weekly buys used) — hunting for a company good enough to deploy it. +1.81% since our $100K start.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — nudged ahead of the market · equity $101,391 · day +0.16% · SPY ~flat
latest_messages.body: Quiet, healthy midday — no buying or selling, all four holdings safe. Risk sweep clean: nothing near its −7% exit (worst is DE −0.8%), and all four winners are already on the smarter trailing safety net — nothing to fix. JPMorgan's trailing stop (upgraded at the open) is already ratcheting up as the stock climbs. Encouraging detail: the market gave back its morning pop and drifted to flat, while GE and JPMorgan firmed — so we edged a hair ahead today. Still no new company clears our strict quality bar, so I'm not forcing a buy (AXP is closest, but not until its Jul 24 report). GE reports tomorrow morning — the event I'm watching; I'll hold through it. Deere has the tightest cushion (~1.7%) — watching into the close. Full scorecard at 3pm. +1.39% since our $100K start.

latest_messages.headline: Open: upgraded JPMorgan's safety net · equity $101,431 · day +0.20% · SPY +0.41%
latest_messages.body: One good action at the open: JPMorgan crossed $346, the level I flagged yesterday, so I upgraded its safety net to a trailing stop (an auto-sell that rises with the price to lock in gains, never dropping back). No buying or selling — pure protection. All four holdings now use this smarter safety net. JPMorgan's strong earnings + dividend hike yesterday pushed it over the +5% line that triggers the upgrade. On buying: still nothing clears our strict quality bar (12th empty scan) — June inflation came in cool (good for stocks broadly) but that's not a single-name buy signal; American Express is closest on the bench but not there until its July 24 report. GE reports tomorrow morning — the event I'm watching. Up +0.20% today vs SPY +0.41% (~44% cash is the small drag). +1.43% since our $100K start.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,209 · day −0.16% · SPY +0.38% · alpha −0.54%
latest_messages.body: Quiet, slightly-down day — we ended −0.16% while the S&P rose +0.38%, trailing it by ~0.54% today, all of it cash drag (~44% idle earns nothing on an up day). No trades: the Jul 14 gate day (JPMorgan earnings + June inflation) passed with no verifiable numbers to act on for the 3rd straight session, so I held rather than gamble. Our stocks were fine — Deere recovered off its midday dip, GE and LLY held gains, all four safety nets resting. This week we're −0.60% vs the market's −0.40% (2 days in) — nearly neck-and-neck. Watching GE's Thursday earnings and JPMorgan's ~1.4%-away safety-net upgrade. +1.21% since our $100K start.
